paper.title: A hypervelocity star with a Magellanic origin

extraction.summary: 26 previously known hypervelocity stars re-assessed with Gaia DR2 proper motions. HVS3 (HE 0437-5439) uniquely identified as originating from the LMC with ejection velocity 870 km/s, providing strong direct evidence for a massive black hole (>=4e3-1e4 Msun) in the LMC. Most other HVSs more consistent with MW origin. Table 1 provides RA, Dec, pmRA, pmDec, v_hel, D_hel for all 26 stars.

method_chain.summary: 26 previously known HVSs with positions, radial velocities, and distances from literature. Gaia DR2 proper motions for most stars (except HVS1, HVS4, HVS13, HIP60350 with HST). References: Brown+2015, Gaia DR2, Geier+2015, Brown+2014, Zheng+2014, Huang+2017, Heber+2008, Irrgang+2010.

source_refs.context: Tab. 1: 26 HVSs, proper motions from Gaia DR2 + HST

method_chain.summary: Two MW potentials: MWPotential2014 (NFW halo + Miyamoto-Nagai disk + power-law bulge) and Piffl+2017 model. LMC: Hernquist profile, M=1.5e11 Msun, r_s=17.14 kpc. Dynamical friction on LMC included. Solar: R0=8.3±0.1 kpc, (U,V,W)=(11.1,24,7.3) km/s.

source_refs.context: Sec 2.1: MWPotential2014 + Piffl+2017, LMC M=1.5e11 Msun

method_chain.summary: MC sampling (N varies: up to 100k for HVS3) of 6D phase space + LMC phase space + solar position. Rewind each HVS 500 Myr. Record closest approach distance, relative velocity, lookback time to MW and LMC. Disk plane crossing locations recorded.
outputs: Closest approach distances and times to MW and LMC for each HVS

source_refs.context: Sec 2.2: MC sampling, rewind 500 Myr, closest approach to MW and LMC

method_chain.summary: Determine probability of origin by computing P(r_min < 5 kpc) for MW and LMC. HVS3: P(LMC)=0.40 vs P(MW)=1e-4. Ejection velocity 870(+69/-66) km/s, time 21.1(+6.1/-4.6) Myr. Mock tests show false positive rate ~10^-3.
inputs: Mock HVS samples from N-body simulations

source_refs.context: Mock tests: misclassification probability ~1/1000 with Gaia DR2

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS1: Previously known hypervelocity star, re-assessed with Gaia DR2 proper motions by Erkal et al. (2019). RA=09:07:44.99, Dec=+02:45:06.9, v_hel=831.1 ± 5.7 km/s, D_hel=107 ± 15 kpc. Prototype HVS, unbound B star at 107 kpc.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper integrates Gaia DR2 proper motions to rewind orbits of 26 previously known HVSs in the combined Milky Way + LMC potential. Monte Carlo sampling of 6D phase space with observational uncertainties. Stars tabulated with RA, Dec, pm, v_hel, D_hel in Table 1.

source_refs.context: Abstract: 26 previously known HVSs, Gaia DR2 proper motions
Sec 2.2: MC sampling of 6d phase space, rewind 500 Myr
Tab. 1: 26 HVSs with coordinates, proper motions, velocities, distances
HVS1-24, US708, HE0437-5439, HD271791, HIP60350, LAMOST-HVS1-3 are previously known

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS2: Previously known hypervelocity star, re-assessed with Gaia DR2 proper motions by Erkal et al. (2019). RA=09:33:20.86, Dec=+44:17:05.4, v_hel=917 ± 7 km/s, D_hel=8.5 ± 1 kpc. sdO star US708, fastest known HVS, SN Ia survivor.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper integrates Gaia DR2 proper motions to rewind orbits of 26 previously known HVSs in the combined Milky Way + LMC potential. Monte Carlo sampling of 6D phase space with observational uncertainties. Stars tabulated with RA, Dec, pm, v_hel, D_hel in Table 1.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS3: Previously known hypervelocity star, re-assessed with Gaia DR2 proper motions by Erkal et al. (2019). RA=04:38:12.8, Dec=-54:33:12, v_hel=723 ± 3 km/s, D_hel=61 ± 10 kpc. HE 0437-5439. Originates from LMC! v_ej=870 km/s, t_ej=21.1 Myr. Requires IMBH in LMC.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper integrates Gaia DR2 proper motions to rewind orbits of 26 previously known HVSs in the combined Milky Way + LMC potential. Monte Carlo sampling of 6D phase space with observational uncertainties. Stars tabulated with RA, Dec, pm, v_hel, D_hel in Table 1.

origin_site.raw_value: Centre of the Large Magellanic Cloud

source_refs.context: HVS3 is consistent with being ejected from the very center of the LMC

origin_classification.raw_value: Hills mechanism ejection by massive black hole in LMC (M_BH >= 4e3-1e4 Msun)

source_refs.context: Ejection velocity 871 km/s requires M_BH >= 4e3-1e4 Msun
